summary: Finished the remaining ftSamus decomp work by moving a large block of Samus-specific assembly into C, adding a new ftsamus6.c translation unit, wiring the decompiled functions into Samus action-state tables and call sites, and expanding Samus special-attribute/header definitions needed by the new C. The PR removed about 1008 lines from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftsamus.s and added C implementations for functions such as ftSamus_8012A96C, ftSamus_8012AC00, ftSamus_8012ADF0, ftSamus_SpecialLw_StartAction, ftSamus_SpecialAirLw_StartAction, and helper callbacks. There was no recorded review discussion in the slice.
what_changed: Replaced most of asm/melee/ft/chara/ftsamus.s with decompiled C, leaving only a small assembly stub area and action-state data references.

Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftSamus/ftsamus6.c with 226 lines implementing later Samus routines, including ftSamus_8012ADF0, ftSamus_8012AEBC, ftSamus_8012AF38, ftSamus_SpecialLw_StartAction, ftSamus_SpecialAirLw_StartAction, ftSamus_8012B150, ftSamus_8012B264, ftSamus_8012B358, ftSamus_8012B3A8, ftSamus_8012B41C, ftSamus_8012B488, ftSamus_8012B4FC, ftSamus_8012B570, ftSamus_8012B5F0, and ftSamus_8012B668.

Expanded ftsamus5.c with C implementations for functions formerly labeled lbl_8012A96C through lbl_8012ACF8, covering stick-direction checks, ftcmd/state variable transitions, ground/air transitions, ledge/ground collision checks, and cleanup through ftSamus_DestroyAllUnsetx2444.
Updated Samus action-state table entries from anonymous assembly labels such as lbl_8012A96C, lbl_8012AB0C, lbl_8012AC00, lbl_8012B150, and lbl_8012B4FC to named ftSamus_* C symbols.
Renamed call sites in existing Samus code and asm from func_8012AEBC/func_8012AF38 to ftSamus_8012AEBC/ftSamus_8012AF38.
Added ftsamus6.c to obj_files.mk so the new translation unit participates in the build.
Extended ftSamusAttributes in ftsamus.h by replacing filler fields with named offset fields x5C through x80, including Vec x74_vec and height_attributes alignment.
Added struct UNK_SAMUS_S2 in ftsamus.h to model the temporary data passed to func_8007B5AC.
Added M_PI_2 to include/math.h and used it for func_80075AF0 angle setup instead of relying on anonymous double constants.
Added an EnvColl_CheckGroundAndLedge declaration in include/functions.h and adjusted duplicated declarations/includes in neighboring fighter files.
smart_moves: Kept the migrated functions address-searchable by using ftSamus_8012xxxx names rather than leaving anonymous lbl_ symbols.
Converted action-state callback table entries at the same time as the C functions, which made the decompiled code immediately referenced by the state machine rather than dead code.
Modeled Samus special attributes by offset names and a Vec field, allowing existing load-scaling code to become clearer, e.g. changing x78 scaling to x74_vec.y.

Introduced a local temporary struct for the func_8007B5AC call instead of passing raw stack bytes, making the stack layout explicit enough for matching while still acknowledging the structure was not fully understood.
Used exact constants such as 0.0f, 1.0f, 3.0f, and M_PI_2 in decompiled calls to avoid depending on removed sdata labels.

Preserved ftcmd/state variable choreography around x2200_ftcmd_var0, x2204_ftcmd_var1, x2208_ftcmd_var2, x2210_ThrowFlags.b0, and x2340_stateVar1, which was central to matching the animation command flow.
decomp_lessons: When finishing a fighter file, update the action-state table, old assembly branch references, headers, and obj_files.mk in the same PR; otherwise newly decompiled functions can compile but remain disconnected.

Offset-based attribute naming is acceptable during decomp when semantic names are not yet known, but replacing filler with typed fields such as Vec x74_vec can unlock clearer code and matching stack/register behavior.
For fighter callbacks, keep the callback slot evidence explicit: this PR sets fighter->cb.x21BC_callback_Accessory4 to ftSamus_8012ADF0 and clears it after the callback fires.
For state-machine decomp, preserve the exact ftcmd and state-variable transitions before renaming for semantics; the matching evidence here is mostly numeric/offset-based.
Shared engine functions discovered during fighter work should be promoted to common headers only with the signatures needed by the matched code, as with EnvColl_CheckGroundAndLedge(HSD_GObj*, s32).
naming_conventions: Newly decompiled Samus functions use the project pattern ftSamus_8012xxxx when the real semantic name is unknown.
Known action entry points retain semantic names such as ftSamus_SpecialLw_StartAction and ftSamus_SpecialAirLw_StartAction.
Samus special-attribute fields use offset names such as x5C, x60, x64, x6C, x70, x74_vec, and x80 when semantics are uncertain.
The temporary hurtbox-related struct is explicitly marked unknown as UNK_SAMUS_S2, signaling incomplete semantic understanding while preserving layout.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed a large contiguous assembly block from ftsamus.s and replaced action-state table .4byte label references with C symbols to preserve table layout.
Used M_PI_2 * fighter->x2C_facing_direction to match the prior lfd/fmul/frsp angle sequence feeding func_80075AF0.
Carried unused parameters in ftSamus_8012B150 and ftSamus_8012B264; the diff comment notes stack sensitivity: 'what the heck is up with the stack in this function... can't get rid of unused params'.
Kept explicit float comparisons and state checks, including x894_currentAnimFrame == 3.0f and x80_self_vel.y >= 0.0f, rather than abstracting them away.

Used helper calls visible in the assembly, including func_8000B1CC, func_802B4AC8, func_8007B0C0, func_8007B5AC, EnvColl_CheckGroundAndLedge, func_80081298, func_80081370, func_80082888, and func_800824A0.
Preserved ground/air branch structure with GA_Air checks and direct calls to func_80084104, func_80084F3C, func_80084DB0, func_800851C0, and func_80081D0C.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename ftSamus_8012AEBC, ftSamus_8012AF38, or UNK_SAMUS_S2 to semantic names.
Check whether the stack workaround comment in ftSamus_8012B150/ftSamus_8012B264 was later removed or explained.
Search for later cleanup of Samus special-attribute fields x5C through x80 and x74_vec.
Compare this PR's action-state table conversion pattern with other fighter completion PRs.
